title: NEPA Environmental Assessment Authority Rule Pack V0.3
description: Authority-driven v1 rule pack. The workflow first identifies applicable statutes, regulations, policies, plans, and state requirements, then evaluates the EA package against each applicable authority with direct source-library citations.
jurisdiction: USDA Forest Service Region 1 source-library corpus

rules.title: National Environmental Policy Act applies
rules.question: Does the EA package show that the review is being conducted under NEPA?
rules.requirement: The EA package should show that the agency is conducting environmental review under the National Environmental Policy Act.
rules.package_query: National Environmental Policy Act NEPA environmental assessment
rules.source_query: National Environmental Policy Act 42 U.S.C. chapter 55 environmental assessment
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NEPA section 4332 environmental information is addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package disclose environmental information, alternatives, consultation, or public availability consistent with NEPA section 4332?
rules.requirement: The EA package should disclose environmental information and analysis needed for informed agency decision-making.
rules.package_query: environmental effects alternatives consultation public comments
rules.source_query: 42 U.S.C. 4332 environmental information alternatives consultation public comments
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NEPA level of review is supported
rules.question: Does the EA package support the chosen level of NEPA review?
rules.requirement: The EA package should support use of an EA by addressing whether significant effects are expected or whether an EIS is required.
rules.package_query: environmental assessment finding of no significant impact significant effects
rules.source_query: 42 U.S.C. 4336 procedure for determination of level of review environmental assessment
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NEPA timing and unified-review duties are addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package document schedule, review management, page-limit, or unified-review considerations?
rules.requirement: The EA package should document review management, schedule, and unified-review considerations where they are part of the NEPA record.
rules.package_query: schedule page limit unified review objection period public circulated
rules.source_query: 42 U.S.C. 4336a timely and unified Federal reviews environmental assessment
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Programmatic or tiered NEPA reliance is supported
rules.question: If the EA tiers to or incorporates a programmatic document, does the package identify that reliance?
rules.requirement: When an EA tiers to or incorporates programmatic NEPA analysis, the reliance should be identified and traceable.
rules.package_query: tiers to incorporates by reference programmatic final environmental impact statement
rules.source_query: 42 U.S.C. 4336b programmatic environmental documents tiering reevaluation
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: USDA EA requirements are met
rules.question: Does the EA package include the core environmental assessment content required by USDA NEPA regulations?
rules.requirement: The EA package should describe the proposal, alternatives as appropriate, affected environment, and environmental effects.
rules.package_query: purpose need alternatives affected environment environmental effects
rules.source_query: 7 CFR 1b.5 environmental assessments purpose need alternatives environmental effects
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: USDA FONSI support is traceable
rules.question: If the package includes a FONSI or decision notice, is the no-significant-impact determination supported?
rules.requirement: A FONSI or decision notice should be traceable to the EA analysis and mitigation or design features relied on for the finding.
rules.package_query: finding of no significant impact FONSI decision notice mitigation rationale
rules.source_query: 7 CFR 1b.6 finding of no significant impact mitigation
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Efficient and integrated environmental review is documented
rules.question: Does the EA package document integrated review, coordination, or efficient environmental review practices?
rules.requirement: The EA package should document coordination and efficient environmental review practices where relevant to the project record.
rules.package_query: integrated review consultation coordination environmental review
rules.source_query: 7 CFR 1b.9 efficient and effective environmental reviews consultation coordination
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NFMA forest-plan consistency is addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package address consistency with the applicable land management plan under NFMA?
rules.requirement: Projects and activities authorized by the Forest Service should be consistent with applicable land management plan direction.
rules.package_query: National Forest Management Act Land Management Plan Forest Plan consistency
rules.source_query: 16 U.S.C. 1604 National Forest Management Act project consistency land management plan
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 36 CFR 219.15 project consistency is addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package describe how the project is consistent with applicable plan components?
rules.requirement: The project approval document should describe consistency with applicable plan components under 36 CFR 219.15.
rules.package_query: 36 CFR 219.15 Land Management Plan Forest Plan plan consistency
rules.source_query: 36 CFR 219.15 project and activity consistency with the plan
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 2022 Custer Gallatin Land Management Plan is applied
applies_if_package_terms: Custer Gallatin National Forest Land Management Plan
rules.question: If the project is on the Custer Gallatin National Forest, does the EA package apply the 2022 Custer Gallatin Land Management Plan?
rules.requirement: The EA package should identify and apply the forest plan that governs the project area.
rules.package_query: Custer Gallatin 2022 Land Management Plan plan consistency
package_terms: Custer Gallatin National Forest Land Management Plan
rules.source_query: 2022 Custer Gallatin Land Management Plan project and activity consistency
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Endangered Species Act section 7 consultation is addressed
rules.question: If listed species or ESA consultation are implicated, does the EA package address ESA section 7?
rules.requirement: The EA package should address ESA section 7 consultation when listed species or critical habitat may be affected.
rules.package_query: Endangered Species Act Section 7 threatened endangered biological assessment
rules.source_query: ESA section 7 16 U.S.C. 1536 consultation threatened endangered species
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: ESA consultation regulations are addressed
rules.question: If ESA consultation is implicated, does the EA package address the consultation process?
rules.requirement: The EA package should identify the consultation path and effects determinations when ESA consultation is implicated.
rules.package_query: Endangered Species Act Section 7 biological assessment Fish and Wildlife Service consultation
rules.source_query: 50 CFR part 402 interagency cooperation endangered species consultation biological assessment
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NHPA section 106 compliance is addressed
rules.question: If historic properties or cultural resources are implicated, does the EA package address NHPA section 106?
rules.requirement: The EA package should address section 106 consultation and findings when historic properties may be affected.
rules.package_query: National Historic Preservation Act Section 106 SHPO cultural resources historic properties
rules.source_query: NHPA section 106 54 U.S.C. 306108 historic properties consultation
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 36 CFR part 800 consultation process is addressed
rules.question: If section 106 is implicated, does the EA package address the 36 CFR part 800 process?
rules.requirement: The EA package should trace SHPO or THPO consultation, effects findings, and mitigation when 36 CFR part 800 applies.
rules.package_query: 36 CFR part 800 Section 106 SHPO adverse effect historic properties
rules.source_query: 36 CFR part 800 protection of historic properties adverse effect consultation
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Montana SHPO review is addressed
rules.question: If Montana SHPO review is implicated, does the EA package document that review?
rules.requirement: The EA package should document Montana SHPO review, concurrence, or consultation when cultural resources are implicated in Montana.
rules.package_query: Montana SHPO State Historic Preservation Officer concurrence cultural resources
rules.source_query: Montana SHPO project review compliance cultural resources
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Roadless Rule compliance is addressed
rules.question: If inventoried roadless areas are implicated, does the EA package address the Roadless Rule?
rules.requirement: The EA package should address roadless-area effects and consistency where inventoried roadless areas are implicated.
rules.package_query: Roadless Rule inventoried roadless area roadless characteristics
rules.source_query: 36 CFR part 294 subpart B 2001 roadless rule inventoried roadless areas
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Executive Order 11990 wetlands protection is addressed
rules.question: If wetlands are implicated, does the EA package address Executive Order 11990?
rules.requirement: The EA package should address wetlands protection, avoidance, minimization, or compensating measures when wetlands are implicated.
rules.package_query: Executive Order 11990 wetlands wetland protection mitigation
rules.source_query: Executive Order 11990 protection of wetlands avoid minimize preserve
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Migratory Bird Treaty Act compliance is addressed
rules.question: If migratory birds are implicated, does the EA package address MBTA responsibilities?
rules.requirement: The EA package should address migratory bird effects or conservation measures when migratory birds may be affected.
rules.package_query: Migratory Bird Treaty Act migratory bird conservation measures
rules.source_query: Migratory Bird Treaty Act 16 U.S.C. 703 migratory birds
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Executive Order 13186 migratory-bird responsibilities are addressed
rules.question: If migratory birds are implicated, does the EA package address Executive Order 13186 responsibilities?
rules.requirement: The EA package should address agency responsibilities to protect migratory birds when they are implicated.
rules.package_query: Executive Order 13186 migratory bird responsibilities conservation
rules.source_query: Executive Order 13186 responsibilities of federal agencies to protect migratory birds
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.
